skill.name: cal-com-automation
skill.description: Automate Cal.com scheduling workflows through Rube MCP, including bookings, availability, webhooks, teams, and organization lookup.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to manage Cal.com bookings, availability, webhooks, teams, and organization details.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer over-reported shell execution because Markdown backticks contain MCP tool names and Cal.com parameter names, not executable Ruby or shell code. Real risk remains because the skill directs users to connect an external Rube MCP endpoint and perform Cal.com account operations, including bookings, webhooks, teams, and organization lookup. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Service Controls Cal.com Operations
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for external service risk. The skill instructs users to add the Rube MCP endpoint and authenticate a Cal.com connection, so scheduling data and account actions depend on a third-party MCP service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The setup flow explicitly names an external MCP URL and Cal.com authentication. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it creates third-party data handling and account-control risk.

medium_findings.title: Cal.com Account Mutation Workflows
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for operational risk. The skill guides assistants to create bookings, update or delete webhooks, create teams, and retrieve organization details through Cal.com MCP tools.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequences include actions that modify scheduling, webhook, and team state. The behavior is intended, but mistakes or overbroad permissions can affect a real Cal.com account.

medium_findings.title: Webhook Configuration Can Expose Booking Events
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for data exposure risk. The skill supports webhook subscriber URLs, event triggers, and webhook secrets, which can route booking events to external endpoints if configured incorrectly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The webhook workflow explicitly includes subscriberUrl, eventTriggers, active status, and secret parameters. This is normal webhook functionality, but it can disclose event metadata to chosen endpoints.

low_findings.title: Backticked Tool Names Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick hits are Markdown formatting around MCP tool names, parameter names, and workflow examples, not commands executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md contains documentation only, and the marked lines use inline Markdown code formatting. No Ruby code, shell command, eval path, or script file is present.

low_findings.title: Configuration and Environment Access Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The env_access hit points to a Cal.com booking status filter description, not process environment access or credential loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 39 documents a status parameter and contains no environment variable access. No evidence of process.env, dotenv, or configuration-file parsing was found in the skill source.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines describe the skill and a webhook setup step; they do not define hashing, encryption, signing algorithms, or weak crypto usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain prose about Cal.com automation and webhook setup. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptographic implementation appears in the file.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Documentation Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The system and network reconnaissance hits are parameter notes, permission notes, or quick-reference table entries for Cal.com tool usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explain time zones, identifiers, permissions, and webhook operations. They do not instruct host probing, network scanning, account discovery outside Cal.com, or system enumeration.

low_findings.title: Critical Combined-Heuristic Finding Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The combination of code execution, network, and credential access is not present; the file is Markdown guidance for MCP tool calls and contains no executable implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network use and webhook secret terminology are legitimate for this Cal.com automation skill. The code-execution and credential-access portions of the heuristic are not supported by file evidence.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Network Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This is required for functionality, but users should understand the third-party network dependency.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is explicitly listed in setup instructions. The risk is contextual rather than malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Account-Changing MCP Tool Workflows
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents tools that can create bookings, update webhooks, delete webhooks, create teams, and retrieve organization identifiers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced tool sequences are clear and specific. They are legitimate Cal.com workflows but can change production data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Webhook Data Routing
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill supports subscriber URLs, event triggers, active flags, and webhook secrets, which can route scheduling event data outside Cal.com.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The webhook parameters and workflow are explicitly documented. Exposure depends on the user-selected endpoint and permissions.

content.value_statement: Manual Cal.com administration slows booking, availability, webhook, and team updates. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for structured Cal.com operations.
actual_capabilities: Searches Rube MCP for current Cal.com tool schemas before use.
Lists and creates Cal.com bookings with status and date filters.
Checks busy times and available booking slots for event types.
Lists, retrieves, updates, and deletes Cal.com webhooks.
Lists teams, retrieves team details, creates teams, and lists team event types.
Retrieves the Cal.com organization ID for organization-level workflows.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP access and an active Cal.com connection.
Cannot work without the permissions granted to the connected Cal.com account.
Does not provide Cal.com API schemas inside the skill; it instructs tool schema search first.
Webhook and booking changes can affect production scheduling data.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Client Bookings
use_cases.description: Find available Cal.com slots, create bookings, and confirm appointment details without manually navigating scheduling screens.

use_cases.title: Maintain Booking Webhooks
use_cases.description: Review webhook settings, update event triggers, and remove obsolete webhook endpoints from a Cal.com account.

use_cases.title: Administer Team Scheduling
use_cases.description: List teams, inspect team event types, and create team scheduling structures when organization permissions allow it.

prompt_templates.title: List Upcoming Bookings
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Cal.com automation skill to list my upcoming bookings for the next seven days. Search Rube MCP tools first.

prompt_templates.title: Find Available Slots
prompt_templates.prompt: Check available slots for event type [event type ID] between [start date] and [end date] in [timezone]. Present the best options before booking.

prompt_templates.title: Review Webhook Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: List my Cal.com webhooks, identify inactive or duplicate endpoints, and recommend safe updates before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Team Scheduling Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect my Cal.com teams and team event types, then propose the exact team or webhook changes needed for [workflow goal]. Ask before applying mutations.

output_examples.input: Find available times for a customer demo next week.
output: The assistant checks the current Cal.com tool schema through Rube MCP.
It retrieves busy times and available slots for the requested event type.
It presents available times with timezone labels and waits for selection.

output_examples.input: Audit my Cal.com webhooks for booking events.
output: The assistant lists configured webhooks and summarizes active triggers.
It flags inactive, duplicate, or unexpected subscriber endpoints.
It recommends updates and asks before changing or deleting webhooks.

output_examples.input: Set up scheduling for a new support team.
output: The assistant retrieves organization and team details first.
It confirms whether the connected account can create teams.
It proposes team names, slugs, and event type checks before changes.

best_practices: Search Rube MCP tools before each workflow because Cal.com schemas can change.
Confirm event type IDs, time zones, and booking times before creating bookings.
Ask for explicit approval before updating webhooks, deleting webhooks, or creating teams.
anti_patterns: Do not create bookings without first checking availability for the selected event type.
Do not send webhook events to unverified subscriber URLs.
Do not assume organization or team permissions are available for every Cal.com account.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Cal.com API key?
faq.answer: The skill expects Rube MCP and an active Cal.com connection. It does not include or request a hardcoded API key.

faq.question: Can it create bookings automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, it documents booking creation through Cal.com MCP tools. Users should confirm slot, attendee, and timezone details first.

faq.question: Can it manage webhooks?
faq.answer: Yes, it covers listing, retrieving, updating, and deleting Cal.com webhooks when the connected account has permission.

faq.question: Can it manage teams?
faq.answer: Yes, it documents listing teams, reading team details, creating teams, and retrieving team event types.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation-only, but it connects to an external MCP endpoint and can change real Cal.com scheduling data.

faq.question: Does the skill contain executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed source is a Markdown skill file with MCP workflow instructions and no executable script files.
